sentences.text: Good morning, Catherine, how you doing.
Talk to me a little bit about this Cortera deal.
It sounds pretty big sounds pretty huge.
yeah, it was a big one.
Took a while to get it over the finish line.
went through like, not a merger, but they basically took all of their management from in-house to Rkw.
And Alfred, I think, is the like umbrella for Rkw.
So it got really held up over the last honestly, like 3 months, because they were about to sign it at the end of March, and then all of this due diligence stuff was going on.
They couldn't about it, and they didn't know if they could sign it still.
And then the deal closed, and they were like, we still don't know who's gonna sign it.
Yeah, it was fun, but they did it.
It sounds like there were a lot of moving parts.
It was a long one, but those always feel really good when you finally get them done.
Yeah, so do we have a defined idea of when they want to start.
Are they kind of like Asap.
And I guess my question is like, How will this interact with the existing Rkw.
That final question you just asked.

I think they will operate pretty separately, honestly, because the team that we sold to for this deal has been the asset management team and the investments team and our main point of contact is named Sam Frida, FRED.
He's the managing director of asset management.
He lives in Orange County, California.
But they're based in Charlotte, North Carolina.
And we had a different contact who actually didn't survive the transition in terms of his role.
He was like overseeing all of their it and new technology.
So we were working with him up until about the beginning of April but Sam, so Sam has been on all of our calls.
We had a really active pilot with them, with a lot of users.
and Sam was always on all the calls, but not really using the product so much as like gathering the feedback from his team.
And he was really really intense on all the calls.
and we just kind of go down these technical rabbit hole rabbit holes with questions.

And Corey and I were in Orange County, visiting another prospect a few weeks ago, and so I was like, we have to go to lunch with him, at least, or something, and we were kind of not sure how that would go, cause he's like so intense on all these calls.
He is the nicest, like most fun, Guy, and it was.
So weird because he's like so intense on calls.
But he's actually so great in person.
So I don't know whether he'll be OP tech, or if if we'll find some way for you to get to meet him at some point.
But he's really really really nice.
To hear that to be honest.
To get it over the line and everything.
So I would say, like he might seem intense on calls.
But I think if you like.
ask more questions or try to get to know him a little bit.
I think he'll loosen up some.
So he's just super locked in on these calls.
and he talks about them a lot.
And then his team has also been really active.
Still, I think we still have their pilot live until we get everything built.
So I'm just seeing how many people are in there.
and they have 25 of them active and.
They buy more on the on when they signed?
Or is this kind of like their cap right now?
No, I think this is their cap right now.
And I do believe we included, just explore basic.
I only ask because we got a slack this morning from George saying that any of those premium things could be delayed.
So they're asking like, Hey, did anybody get promised this otherwise?
Because they're not getting it today?
Okay, so their their contract does not have explore and research on it.
Okay, what else do I need to know?
That is a lot of wonderful background information, Catherine.
What is their thoughts on the go live of the whole portfolio.
What have they kind of communicated to you on their expectations?
I gave the 2 week timeline but I did email him yesterday because Alex Field has been asking me for a property list with the addresses of the properties.
So I asked Sam for that yesterday and asked him about.
I think I asked him about times for Kickoff call.
Oh, no, I just said we would be setting it up later this week.
it's crazy that it's only Tuesday.
It feels like it should be like Thursday or something.
Oh, my God, yeah, I don't know if you're also suffering from the heat down in Nashville, but for us up here in the northeast.
It is so nasty the last 2, 3 days.
It just feels like it feels miserable, and everything is moving slowly.
I forget that you're in the northeast.
For some reason I always want to think that you're in Dallas.
That's kind of cool, though I'd love to give Dallas vibes.
I spent a lot of my childhood in North Jersey.
I'm gonna make that note, too.
Got out to the West Coast from Jersey.
I can follow up with him.
Maybe I give him today, and then I'll make a note to follow up tomorrow morning.
Just so I can start kind of get in the loop, and then, if you don't want to officially introduce me until the kickoff.

But I just wanna make sure that, like you and I are both aware of when this guy is going to send over those property addresses, because if we quoted him 2 weeks, I think without those addresses, it's gonna be very hard to hit that.
So just wanna make sure we're on top of him.
Yep, I will send that tomorrow morning if we don't hear from him today, and then I don't know who he'll have on that other than him.
It might be a big group, it might not.
But the thing that will be nice with this one is, they're already pretty well versed in how to use it, because they've been using it since.
So like a nice core user group.
and I don't think I don't know exactly how it will work with their site teams getting access if that will be through Alfred and Rkw.
But I don't know that we're gonna need to do a lot of additional training with them.
I feel a lot better about the 2 week turnaround.
I think that's all I need to know, for now.
I don't think they're gonna be like overly demanding.
I think they'll be pretty like
off on their own using it.

So that sounds good to me?
I'd be a rich man if I had even a nickel for each of those.
Yeah, I think you'll like them.
Well done, and thank you for the the easy handoff here.

Let me know if there's anything else that comes up or anything else that changes.
But for 45 properties and no real additional user training lift.
I think we could certainly accommodate that 2 week turnaround very comfortably.
